stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The inventor Elias Howe patented the lockstitch sewing machine in 1846.
  • He patented it in Massachusetts.
  • The lockstitch was the first widely successful sewing machine mechanism.
  • The town of Howe, Indiana, is named after Elias Howe.
  • Howe Street in New Haven, Connecticut, is also named after him.
stem: The student wants to indicate how Howe, Indiana, got its name. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Howe, Indiana, is named after Elias Howe, patent holder of the first widely successful sewing machine mechanism.
Howe, Indiana, shares its name with Howe Street in New Haven, Connecticut.
Howe Street is in New Haven, Connecticut, where the lockstitch sewing machine was patented in 1846.
Patented in 1846 in Massachusetts, the lockstitch was the first widely successful sewing machine mechanism.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence indicates how Howe, Indiana, got its name, noting that the town was named after Elias Howe, the patent holder of the first widely successful sewing machine mechanism.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence indicates that Howe, Indiana, has the same name as a street in Connecticut; it doesn't indicate how the town of Howe got its name. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence indicates that Howe Street is in New Haven, Connecticut; it doesn't indicate how Howe, Indiana, got its name. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence provides details about Howe's invention; it doesn't indicate how Howe, Indiana, got its name.
